MainFrameMicroIllusions1988Verified sets:2
Archived: 2005-10-26 21:34:06
Updated: 2026-01-18 22:00:41
Protection: Main Frame protection is having cbm track 36 recorded at 4us, but sector 16 at 3.5us. Once you copy the track (or sample in a 1541 with a constant speed) sector 16 becomes permanently corrupt, however that contains the (poorly) encrypted second boot code.
Notes: cifo
Contributor: lyons, jcompton, whocrazy

Archived: 2005-10-26 21:34:06
Updated: 2025-10-16 16:30:08

Archived: 2007-04-24 21:51:49
Updated: 2025-10-16 16:30:08

Contributor: varius
Defender of the CrownCinemaware1987Verified sets:2
Archived: 2005-10-26 21:34:06
Updated: 2025-10-16 16:30:08
Protection: V-MAX! V2A - side 1 boots fine on real disk and emulator, but fails a check of tracks 12-18 on side 2 when you "go raiding". Probably a tracksync check-- this is very similar to protection in Star Rank Boxing and the trick that is pulled out on the "important" titles. tracks 16 and 17 are very long (>8000 bytes) and would need remastered at 295-296RPM.
Notes: cifo - verified
Contributor: many, jerry

Protection: looks for 4 GCR bytes written at non-standard density in tail gap of track 16 sector 20. EOR'd together must = $79.

Beyond the Black HoleSoftware Toolworks1989Verified sets:0
Archived: 2006-03-31 21:26:34
Updated: 2025-10-16 16:30:08
Protection: After the title screen, it reads track 16 sector 20 (which seems to be mostly empty), after the read the drive head is at the tail gap (normally $55 bytes). It reads until it gets a $6A byte, puts it in the $309 location in drive memory. Then it reads the next 4 bytes and puts them reversed at $305-$308. Standard GCR decode follows and data is sent to the C64. The C64 loads the data into $CB00, then EORs the 4 bytes it loaded together. Result must be $79 or reset occurs. Force the value for a crack (I didn't check for checksums or anything like that) and the program continues and asks you to insert side 2, which I don't have. Or fix the GCR bytes in the image so they EOR (after GCR decode) to $79, which shouldn't be too hard. BTW, the non-working bytes in the NIB dump you have are at offset $3DE97 and are: 6A 3D 5C D1 9F This string of bytes appears to be written at a different density level, that's why it's coming in incorrectly. Interesting. (LORD CRASS)
Notes: cifo
Contributor: lyons
ZenjiActivision1984Verified sets:2
Archived: 2006-03-30 18:09:41
Updated: 2025-10-16 16:30:08
Protection: checks e5 on t18s9
Notes: cifo - verified
Contributor: lyons, visegrips, tobias

Real GhostbustersData East1987Verified sets:0
Archived: 2005-10-26 21:34:06
Updated: 2025-10-16 16:30:08
Protection: signature tracks 36-41. it checks for a very small (10-bit) sync then $AC (or other bitshifted variants) on track 36 during load. this sync is too small to detect reliably with BIT $1C00 when imaging, so I had to manually fix the image.
Notes: clean image from original. shortest "crack" possible, I changed literally one single bit.
Contributor:

Elite (PAL)Firebird1985Verified sets:0
Archived: 2022-01-16 00:00:00
Updated: 2025-10-16 16:30:08
Protection: 'Morse Technology Limited' loader -- Floppy-side loader code on t36/s01 and t36/s11. EOR'd with 5. It checks two e5's on t14 (s3 and sector 14). (Courtesy of Roger Cousin)
Notes: works in PAL only. 2 dumps of this are completely different. One has a couple extra bad sectors and has no music in the title screen, so likely a bad dump.

Bridge BaronThomas Throop1985Verified sets:0
Archived: 2005-10-26 21:34:06
Updated: 2025-10-16 16:30:08
Protection: checks track 36 (and probably track 38 later in the game) for an 01010101 sequence immediately after a lone sync on the track. this is all that is on those tracks. there is an illegal GCR nibble just after the sync that causes the image to read in out of framing about 50% of the time, but it doesn't actually check for the bad nibble, only that it has the pattern, so it works on the emulators and remasters after this nibble is corrected. another single bit patch. :)
Notes: clean image from original
Contributor:

Archived: 2007-03-28 21:24:18
Updated: 2025-10-16 16:30:08
Protection: check e5 on t1s5

Notes: cifo - the signature check is too new for 1983- the disk sleeve (which is probably original) says 1985. this disk has the Atari version of the game on the same disk side. several tracks are Atari formatted. This also matches a "VAL-U-LINE" original, so it's a re-release
Contributor: sdahl, fogt, jcompton

Big Box - Big 1 (Compilation) (PAL)Beau-Jolly1989Verified sets:0
Archived: 2007-02-17 11:01:42
Updated: 2025-10-16 16:30:08
Protection: none
Notes: cifo - one entry hardly does this one justice. It's 16 full disk sides worth of games on one compilation:
---
D1S1 - Baseball, Hacker, Alien
D1S2 - Basketball, Mermaid, Enduro Racer, Explorer
D2S1 - Galactic Games, Guadal Canal, Park Patrol
D2S2 - Prodigy, Dandy, Wonderboy
D3S1 - Rampage, Spindizzy, Star Raiders 2
D3S2 - Super Sprint, B.T.L.C., High Frontier
D4S1 - Football
D4S2 - Knightmare
D5S1 - Super Hang-On
D5S2 - Super Hang On 2, GhostBusters
(This side of the disk was notched by some former owner of the game set. All other disks were not notched (for side B, side A was always "factory" notched). Judging from the disk dump layout, that disk side was not rewritten, but was written with the very same machine as all the other disks. So perhaps the disk was notched to be able to save some highscores. The disk ID of this side was the same as for Disc No. 3, side B, but with definately other content.)
D6S1 - Real GhostBusters
D6S2 - Gee Bee Air Rally
D7S1 - Karnov
D7S2 - Firetrap
D8S1 - Corporation
D8S2 - Ace, I.S.S.

Contributor: dc
Tiger Road (PAL)Capcom1987Verified sets:1
Archived: 2007-02-17 11:10:43
Updated: 2025-10-16 16:30:08

Archived: 2008-04-19 14:13:53
Updated: 2025-10-16 16:30:08
Protection: none
Notes: There are several disks that go to this set, including Pro Greats, College Greats, Draft/Trade Ratings, Standings and League Leaders. Some of the disks are (c) 1984 and some are (c) 1987 so some disks may be upgrade stats. Some disks had bad sectors I could not recover, but these are rare, so we may never see another set.

F-14 TomcatActivision1988Verified sets:1
Archived: 2008-04-30 21:27:18
Updated: 2025-10-16 16:30:08
Protection: xemag 2.0 - fat on t35/36
Notes: cifo - verified - this is an alternate version to the other one already archived. it has disk protection instead of manual. there are very little changes to the disk *one data sector, plus some differently formatted unused sectors so its just a protection swap*. also, it does not work in either VICE or CCS, but works remastered. this same release has been found in NTSC and PAL countries

FlakFunsoft1984Verified sets:0
Archived: 2009-01-18 13:33:12
Updated: 2025-10-16 16:30:08
Protection: syncr0l0k - This awful little protection writes to the disk. It writes to t/s 19/02 and that must succeed or it tries over and over. Then it tries writing to t/s 20/02. This must fail or it tries over and over again. The protection seems to be based on physically damaged sectors that need to return errors when attempts are made to write to them. I tried erasing the sector header for 20/02 to see if it would fool the protection, but it doesn't work. The protection checks that some of the sector was written (first 6 bytes at the minimum must match). After it writes to track 20, it reads it back to verify it. The data it writes is a pseudo-random byte taken from the timer, so it's different each time you load the game. When it reads it back, at least 6 bytes must match what it thinks it wrote, but the whole sector cannot match. If it does: protection failed. Unless someone can think of a way to damage the sector in an image to the extent that a U2 write command will only partially work, this won't function in an emulator. And unless you can physically damage a real disk at the right spot, this won't work remastered either. On the other hand, it's an easy crack to remove the protection check entirely. There's only one little method used to hide it (check $179F in the main program for the self-modifying code that hides the protection check at $17A2). (LordCrass)
Notes: cifo
Contributor: hok

Archived: 2009-01-18 14:19:47
Updated: 2025-10-16 16:30:08
Protection: checks e4 on t22s10 - ocean protection

Frankie Crashed on JupiterKingsoft1985Verified sets:0
Archived: 2009-04-04 11:30:27
Updated: 2025-10-16 16:30:08
Protection: Protection reads the track 1, sector 20 data block at a non-standard density (%00 instead of %11). The image was read in using the standard density, so the result is garbage. This track needs to be redumped at the lowest density and the sector 20 data block (head block is fine) pasted into the G64 image for it to work in emulation. (LordCrass)
Notes: cifo - needs redump due to density issues
Contributor: joerg

Contributor: clott
Kwik-Write!Datamost1984Verified sets:0
Archived: 2009-04-19 22:38:41
Updated: 2025-10-16 16:30:08
Protection: Track 15 is non-standard. Only the last sector has a sector header and any data in the data block. This data block is divided into 3 shorter sections by sync marks: The first section has $45 bytes of data and is written at a non-standard density (%10 instead of %11). The second section is written at density %01 and $7D bytes are read in. The third section is written at density %00 and $82 more bytes are read in. The GCR data is then decoded using standard ROM GCR decoding routines. This track needs to be redumped three more times, at density levels 0, 1, and 2. The data could then be merged into the image for a working G64. In short, there's all 4 density levels present on this one track (the sector header is written at standard density %11)
Notes: ifo
Contributor: clott

Microleague Wresting (WWF)Microprose1988Verified sets:0
Archived: 2010-07-12 21:02:29
Updated: 2025-10-16 16:30:08
Protection: checks signature on t35 before match, goes back to main menu if failed and does announcements only. The "signature" on t35 is a small byte variation ($4A 55 73 55) in the tail gap of Sector 0. If you replace the "$73" with "$55", the protection will fail, simple but effective, and very easy to miss if you don't know what you are looking for. BTW: the same string (4A 55 73 55) is found on Track 15 Sector 9. I can only guess the reason it was put there, some kind of subtle hint? maybe the idea was to have the crackers work like mad, chasing their tails, only to realize at the last possible moment that the answer was hiding in plain sight all along? My thinking behind this is that throughout the loading process, track 15 isn't even touched, so it can't be checking for a string match, perhaps the string on t15 is scanned for at a later stage, or when performing certain actions? (Stephen)
Notes: cifo
Contributor: jcompton

Archived: 2010-07-13 18:32:54
Updated: 2025-10-16 16:30:08
Protection: checks signature on track 37

Cop Shocker (PAL)Digital Marketing1989Verified sets:0
Archived: 2011-12-05 19:49:42
Updated: 2025-10-16 16:30:08
Protection: Checks non-standard density within a sector. 1. Seeks to track 35, sector 1 2. Reads 41 GCR bytes after the sync mark, they must all be $5A. 3. Changes to highest density. 4. Read 3 GCR bytes, remembering the 3rd byte. 5. The next 10 GCR bytes must all be the same as that 3rd byte. The image is easily fixed for emulators by making those 11 bytes all the same (ie. $65). Fixing this for writing back to real diskette requires a special writing routine that can write this sector with the required non-standard density.
Notes: cifo
Contributor: nozy
Ernie's Big SplashCBS1985Verified sets:0

Summer Games (NTSC) (Prototype 032784)Epyx1984Verified sets:1
Archived: 2006-04-25 23:14:43
Updated: 2025-10-16 16:30:08
Protection: checks for e11's all over track 35 before every event.
Notes: cifo - verified - this is first early release of the game, and less common. My original has a label that says "PROTOTYPE - NOT A COMPLETE GAME" but it was a store demo and others have been found. It lacks 3 disk errors present later in NTSC and PAL releases of the game.
Contributor: peter

Fist II: The Legend ContinuesMelbourne House1986Verified sets:0
Archived: 2019-03-02 00:00:00
Updated: 2025-10-16 16:30:08
Protection: melbourne protection - very elaborate. checks unformatted tracks at the beginning and end of disk, $55 byte pattern in weak bits on track 20, then track 19 signature pattern with a long sync.
Notes: cifo - game seems the same as "Fist" USA release. Could just be a name change.
